summary: Ukrainian forces struck Russian positions on the southern front with FPV drones, destroying an observation post, fortified shelter, mortar position, and antenna equipment, killing most personnel. Ukraine’s drone procurement doubled year-over-year, with a 17% increase in February deliveries of Mavic, Autel, and Matrice systems. Russian operators reported 104 strikes on March 11, 2026, including FPV air defense against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, while Ukrainian forces destroyed a MaxxPro armored vehicle in Dobropole using a fiber-optic FPV drone. Ukrainian FPV pilots are experimenting with tactics like dismounting Russian troops from horses before strikes, and soldiers report growing use of Russian "Molnias" drones, which evade electronic warfare due to their passive control signal reception.
